tluaIndexKeyArguments.tlua(11,20): error TS100048: A type argument for an index-key type parameter cannot include 'nil'.
tluaIndexKeyArguments.tlua(12,24): error TS100048: A type argument for an index-key type parameter cannot include 'nil'.
tluaIndexKeyArguments.tlua(13,22): error TS100048: A type argument for an index-key type parameter cannot include 'nil'.
tluaIndexKeyArguments.tlua(14,18): error TS100048: A type argument for an index-key type parameter cannot include 'nil'.
tluaIndexKeyArguments.tlua(35,23): error TS100048: A type argument for an index-key type parameter cannot include 'nil'.


==== tluaIndexKeyArguments.tlua (5 errors) ====
    // An EXPLICIT type argument for an index-key type parameter is re-validated
    // against the key domain (any non-nil type) at the reference -- the same rule
    // the declaration site enforces with 100039.
    
    interface Dict<K, V> {
        [key: K]: V;
    }
    
    // Arguments outside the key domain error at the reference, not as a confusing
    // structural mismatch later.
    type BadNil = Dict<nil, number>;
                       ~~~
!!! error TS100048: A type argument for an index-key type parameter cannot include 'nil'.
    type BadUnknown = Dict<unknown, number>;
                           ~~~~~~~
!!! error TS100048: A type argument for an index-key type parameter cannot include 'nil'.
    type BadUnion = Dict<string | nil, number>;
                         ~~~~~~~~~~~~
!!! error TS100048: A type argument for an index-key type parameter cannot include 'nil'.
    local bad: Table<nil, number> | nil;
                     ~~~
!!! error TS100048: A type argument for an index-key type parameter cannot include 'nil'.
    
    // Legal arguments stay legal: a literal widens to its domain (the documented
    // precision loss), and a bare type parameter forwards untouched. `any` is
    // unchecked here as everywhere -- `Table<any, any>` is the only spelling of
    // "any table" under an invariant K, and LuaMetatable's __index uses it.
    type Narrow = Dict<"a", number>;
    type AnyKey = Dict<any, number>;
    local anyTable: Table<any, any> | nil;
    declare function get<K, V>(t: Table<K, V>, key: K): V;
    declare function forward<K, V>(t: Dict<K, V>, key: K): V;
    local m: Table<string, number> | nil;
    
    // A parameter that never keys an index is not gated.
    interface Box<T> {
        value: T;
    }
    type NilBox = Box<nil>;
    
    // An alias whose target keys the index is gated too.
    type Assoc<K> = { [key: K]: boolean };
    type BadAlias = Assoc<nil>;
                          ~~~
!!! error TS100048: A type argument for an index-key type parameter cannot include 'nil'.
    type GoodAlias = Assoc<boolean>;
